title: crepis viscidula subsp. geracioides
intro: Crepis viscidula subsp. geracioides, also known as the Geracioid Hawk's Beard or Geracioid Hawkbit, is a subspecies of the Crepis viscidula plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Crepis viscidula subsp. geracioides can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various forms of consumption to harness its medicinal properties.
